title: State Border Security Reimbursement Act of 2025
short_title: State Border Security Reimbursement Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill requires the federal government to pay back states that have spent their own money on border security. To qualify, a state must have spent more than 2.5 billion dollars on border security in the last ten years. States that qualify need to send their receipts and expenses to the federal government by a certain deadline. The federal government must then pay back the full amount that states spent on border security.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the State Border Security Reimbursement Act of 2025.

sections.title: Section 2  Findings
sections.text: Congress believes the following facts are true. First, keeping the border secure is mainly the federal government's job. Second, because the federal government has failed to do this job properly, the State of Texas has been forced to spend its own money on border security. Third, since the two year budget period from 2008 to 2009, Texas has spent more than three billion two hundred eight million dollars on border security. Here is how much they spent in each two year period. From 2008 to 2009, Texas spent more than one hundred ten million dollars. From 2010 to 2011, Texas spent more than one hundred twenty million dollars. From 2012 to 2013, Texas spent more than two hundred twenty two million one hundred thousand dollars. From 2014 to 2015, Texas spent more than five hundred ten million dollars. From 2016 to 2017, Texas spent more than eight hundred million dollars. From 2018 to 2019, Texas spent more than six hundred sixty three million one hundred thousand dollars. From 2020 to 2021, Texas spent more than seven hundred eighty two million eight hundred thousand dollars. For 2022 to 2023, the Texas Legislative Budget Board is recommending another seven hundred ninety seven million one hundred thousand dollars for border security. Fourth, people living in border states are being taxed twice for the same thing. Fifth, when states have to use their taxpayer money and state budgets to handle public safety duties that should be the federal government's responsibility, this creates an unfair burden on the state.

sections.title: Section 3  Reimbursement
sections.text: Any state that has spent more than two billion five hundred million dollars on border security and enforcement to help federal efforts in the ten years before this law was passed will get all of those costs paid back by the federal government. This applies even if other laws say something different. The governors of states that qualify must submit two things within one hundred eighty days after this law is passed. First, they must provide a complete record of all border security expenses that the state and its cities paid for without federal help. Second, they must provide the total amount of all those expenses. The federal government must pay back these expenses within one year after the state submits its application.
